```spthy
theory SignedToken_With_CertPath
begin

/* Roles and intuition:
   - A (Sender): creates a token containing public parameters (tA, rA, B, sgnData)
     and a confidential part for B (encData), here modeled as a fresh session key k,
     encrypted under B's public encryption key pkB. A signs the hash of the entire
     Info tuple and attaches a "certification path" B->A carrying A's public key.
   - B (Receiver): obtains A's public key from the provided path, verifies the
     signature on the Info, checks the intended recipient field equals its own pk,
     decrypts the confidential part with its private key, and accepts.

   Cryptographic primitives:
   - hashing: h/1
   - signatures: sign/2 and verify/3 (signing builtins)
   - public-key encryption: aenc/2 and adec/2 (asymmetric-encryption builtins)

   Intended security goals:
   - Secrecy of the confidential parameter k unless B's long-term decryption key is revealed.
   - Origin authentication / non-repudiation flavor: if B accepts a token as from A,
     then A previously issued a matching token, unless A's long-term signing key was revealed.

   Notation mapping:
   - A{Info} is modeled as the pair <Info, sign(h(Info), skA)>.
   - Bp[encData] is modeled as aenc(encData, pkB).
   - The "certification path B -> A" is abstracted as a tag carrying pkA: <'pathBA', pkA>.
*/

builtins: hashing, signing, asymmetric-encryption

/* Sender A: signs hash(Info) and includes cert path B->A along with the signed token */
let A(skA, pkB) =
  new rA;                   // fresh nonce to prevent replay/forgery
  new tA;                   // timestamp abstraction
  new sgnData;              // non-confidential parameters
  new k;                    // confidential parameter (e.g., a session key)
  let enc    = aenc(k, pkB) in
  let info   = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, enc> in            // Info = (tA, rA, B, sgnData, Bp[encData])
  let H      = h(info) in
  let sig    = sign(H, skA) in                           // A{Info} = <Info, sign(h(Info), skA)>
  let pathBA = <'pathBA', pk(skA)> in                    // abstract certification path B -> A
  event TokenIssued(pk(skA), pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, k);   // record issuance for authentication lemmas
  event SecretK(k);                                      // track confidential parameter
  out(<pathBA, <info, sig>>)

/* Receiver B: verifies cert path, signature, recipient, and decrypts encData */
let B(skB, pkB) =
  in(msg);
  let <<'pathBA', pkA>, <info, sig>> = msg in
  let <tA, rA, rec, sgnData, enc> = info in
  if rec = pkB then                              // check intended recipient is B
    let H = h(info) in
    if verify(sig, H, pkA) = true then           // verify A's signature on Info
      let k = adec(enc, skB) in                  // decrypt confidential parameters
      event AcceptB(pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, k)

/* Key compromise branches (optional, for conditional security lemmas) */
let CompromiseA(skA) =
  event LtkRevealA(pk(skA)); out(skA)

let CompromiseB(skB) =
  event LtkRevealB(pk(skB)); out(skB)

/* System composition:
   - A has a long-term signing key skA (used only for signatures).
   - B has a long-term decryption key skB (used only for public-key encryption).
   - Public keys are published; compromise branches allow key leakage to the adversary. */
process:
!(
  new skA; out(pk(skA)); event HonestA(pk(skA));
  !(
    new skB; out(pk(skB)); event HonestB(pk(skB));
    (
        !A(skA, pk(skB))
      | !B(skB, pk(skB))
      | CompromiseA(skA)
      | CompromiseB(skB)
    )
  )
)

/* Executability: there exists a run where B accepts A's token */
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB t r s k #i #j.
      TokenIssued(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #j
    & AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #i
    & #j < #i"

/* Secrecy of k: if B accepts, then k is not revealed unless B's decryption key is leaked */
lemma secrecy_of_k:
  "All pkA pkB t r s k #i.
      AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #i
      ==> (not (Ex #j. K(k) @ #j)) | (Ex #rB. LtkRevealB(pkB) @ #rB)"

/* Origin authentication / non-repudiation-style: B's acceptance implies A issued,
   unless A's signing key was compromised. */
lemma origin_authentication:
  "All pkA pkB t r s k #i.
      AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #i
      ==> (Ex #j. TokenIssued(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #j & #j < #i)
          | (Ex #rA. LtkRevealA(pkA) @ #rA)"

end
```

Notes:
- The certification path B -> A is abstracted as a tagged carrier of A’s public key; full PKI validation is out of scope here.
- The confidential parameters are modeled as a fresh key k inside Info, encrypted under B’s public key.

theory SignedToken_With_CertPath
begin

/* Roles and intuition:
   - A (Sender): creates a token containing public parameters (tA, rA, B, sgnData)
     and a confidential part for B (encData), here modeled as a fresh session key k,
     encrypted under B's public encryption key pkB. A signs the hash of the entire
     Info tuple and attaches a "certification path" B->A carrying A's public key.
   - B (Receiver): obtains A's public key from the provided path, verifies the
     signature on the Info, checks the intended recipient field equals its own pk,
     decrypts the confidential part with its private key, and accepts.

   Cryptographic primitives:
   - hashing: h/1
   - signatures: sign/2 and verify/3 (signing builtins)
   - public-key encryption: aenc/2 and adec/2 (asymmetric-encryption builtins)

   Intended security goals:
   - Secrecy of the confidential parameter k unless B's long-term decryption key is revealed.
   - Origin authentication / non-repudiation flavor: if B accepts a token as from A,
     then A previously issued a matching token, unless A's long-term signing key was revealed.

   Notation mapping:
   - A{Info} is modeled as the pair <Info, sign(h(Info), skA)>.
   - Bp[encData] is modeled as aenc(encData, pkB).
   - The "certification path B -> A" is abstracted as a tag carrying pkA: <'pathBA', pkA>.
*/

builtins: hashing, signing, asymmetric-encryption

/* Sender A: signs hash(Info) and includes cert path B->A along with the signed token */
let A(skA, pkB) =
  new rA;                   // fresh nonce to prevent replay/forgery
  new tA;                   // timestamp abstraction
  new sgnData;              // non-confidential parameters
  new k;                    // confidential parameter (e.g., a session key)
  let enc    = aenc(k, pkB) in
  let info   = <tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, enc> in            // Info = (tA, rA, B, sgnData, Bp[encData])
  let H      = h(info) in
  let sig    = sign(H, skA) in                           // A{Info} = <Info, sign(h(Info), skA)>
  let pathBA = <'pathBA', pk(skA)> in                    // abstract certification path B -> A
  event TokenIssued(pk(skA), pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, k);   // record issuance for authentication lemmas
  event SecretK(k);                                      // track confidential parameter
  out(<pathBA, <info, sig>>)

/* Receiver B: verifies cert path, signature, recipient, and decrypts encData */
let B(skB, pkB) =
  in(msg);
  let <<'pathBA', pkA>, <info, sig>> = msg in
  let <tA, rA, rec, sgnData, enc> = info in
  if rec = pkB then                              // check intended recipient is B
    let H = h(info) in
    if verify(sig, H, pkA) = true then           // verify A's signature on Info
      let k = adec(enc, skB) in                  // decrypt confidential parameters
      event AcceptB(pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, k)

/* Key compromise branches (optional, for conditional security lemmas) */
let CompromiseA(skA) =
  event LtkRevealA(pk(skA)); out(skA)

let CompromiseB(skB) =
  event LtkRevealB(pk(skB)); out(skB)

/* System composition:
   - A has a long-term signing key skA (used only for signatures).
   - B has a long-term decryption key skB (used only for public-key encryption).
   - Public keys are published; compromise branches allow key leakage to the adversary. */
process:
!(
  new skA; out(pk(skA)); event HonestA(pk(skA));
  !(
    new skB; out(pk(skB)); event HonestB(pk(skB));
    (
        !A(skA, pk(skB))
      | !B(skB, pk(skB))
      | CompromiseA(skA)
      | CompromiseB(skB)
    )
  )
)

/* Executability: there exists a run where B accepts A's token */
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB t r s k #i #j.
      TokenIssued(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #j
    & AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #i
    & #j < #i"

/* Secrecy of k: if B accepts, then k is not revealed unless B's decryption key is leaked */
lemma secrecy_of_k:
  "All pkA pkB t r s k #i.
      AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #i
      ==> (not (Ex #j. K(k) @ #j)) | (Ex #rB. LtkRevealB(pkB) @ #rB)"

/* Origin authentication / non-repudiation-style: B's acceptance implies A issued,
   unless A's signing key was compromised. */
lemma origin_authentication:
  "All pkA pkB t r s k #i.
      AcceptB(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #i
      ==> (Ex #j. TokenIssued(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, k) @ #j & #j < #i)
          | (Ex #rA. LtkRevealA(pkA) @ #rA)"

end
